mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.5%, the labor market tightens sufficiently to trigger wage growth and consumer spending acceleration, which directly lifts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5% over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in housing starts and industrial production confirm synchronized demand recovery across residential and commercial sectors, expanding equity risk premiums and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy by at least 1.5% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, simultaneous rises in the consumer price index and federal funds rate compress equity valuation multiples through higher discount rates and inflation uncertainty, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
regime_hint: active federal funds rate hiking cycle
failure_condition: Equity risk premiums expand rather than contract when both CPI and FEDFUNDS rise simultaneously, resulting in positive forward returns in over 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales figures reflect immediate consumer spending strength, which translates directly into short-term corporate revenue beats and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02 across all available data periods.

mechanism_narrative: A single-month industrial production surge above 0.8% signals rapid capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, boosting industrial and materials sector earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.8% in a single month
failure_condition: Forward returns average negative or zero following INDPRO surges above 0.8%, indicating supply shocks are being interpreted as inflationary rather than growth-positive.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth exceeding 200,000 and a declining unemployment rate confirm structural labor market tightening, which sustains consumer spending power and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces a negative information ratio over any 4-year out-of-sample test, or forward returns are indistinguishable from random noise.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime where real GDP growth exceeds 3.0% annualized while inflation remains below 2.0%, strong earnings visibility without Fed panic expands equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when GDP growth exceeds 3% and CPI stays below 2%, indicating that late-cycle valuation compression dominates earnings growth.
